title: Mighty Brazilian Wood Ear Mushroom Tart
description: A crisp, herbaceous pescatarian tart packed with wood ear mushrooms and shrimp, perfect for a solo Brazilian dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200C and line a 20cm square baking dish with parchment.
Rehydrate 50g dry wood ear mushrooms by soaking in hot water for 20 minutes, then squeeze out excess liquid and set aside.
Peel and devein 100g shrimp, pat dry and set aside.
In a skillet, heat 5ml sesame oil over medium heat and sauté minced garlic until fragrant, about 30 seconds.

Add the rehydrated mushrooms and shrimp to the skillet, cook until shrimp turn pink, about 3 minutes, then season with 2g salt, 1g white pepper, 10ml soy sauce, and 5ml lemon juice; stir to coat and remove from heat.
Roll out the 200g pastry sheet on a lightly floured surface to 0.5cm thickness, cut into a 20cm square, and place into the prepared dish.
Spoon the shrimp and mushroom mixture evenly over the pastry, pressing gently to settle.
Sprinkle 5g chopped fresh coriander over the filling for a herbal aroma.
Brush the edges of the pastry with beaten egg to seal and give a golden finish.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15 minutes, or until the pastry edges are crisp and golden brown.
Let the tart cool for 5 minutes, then carefully remove from the pan and slice into single servings. Serve warm, optionally garnished with extra coriander and a squeeze of lemon.
